title: Director Project Controls – Data Center Construction
description:
Turner & Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. 
Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programs as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. 
Our capabilities include program, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. 
We are majority-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner & Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier program, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. 

Turner & Townsend
 is seeking an experienced 
Director of Project Controls
 to lead cost, schedule, and risk management for a large-scale data center construction program in 
Beaver County, PA
, while supporting major client accounts and broader business initiatives. This is a senior leadership role responsible for establishing and overseeing robust project controls processes, ensuring accurate forecasting, reporting, and governance across all phases of delivery.
The Director will partner with project leadership, contractors, and stakeholders to maintain transparency, mitigate risks, and drive performance against budget and schedule targets. You will lead project controls teams, ensure best practices are implemented, and act as a trusted advisor to clients.

Responsibilities
Establish and execute the strategic direction for project controls, including cost, schedule, and risk management, for a large-scale data center program.
Develop and implement governance frameworks, reporting protocols, and project controls processes to ensure accuracy and compliance.
Oversee budgeting, forecasting, earned value analysis, variance reporting, and lifecycle cost estimating; validate estimates and track actuals against forecasts.
Lead development and integration of detailed CPM schedules (Primavera P6/MS Project) across design, procurement, construction, and commissioning; monitor critical path and implement recovery plans.
Define and manage risk registers; lead risk workshops, establish mitigation strategies, and coordinate contingency planning, including Monte Carlo analyses.
Manage change control processes, including evaluation of scope changes, impact analysis, and cost reduction initiatives.
Prepare executive-level dashboards, gateway documentation, and reports for senior leadership, highlighting performance, risks, and corrective actions.
Ensure alignment on cost and schedule objectives through collaboration with project managers, finance teams, and contractors; support client reporting and governance meetings.
Drive data integrity and reporting accuracy across all phases; utilize project controls tools and platforms (e.g., Primavera P6, MS Project, cost systems).
Develop and maintain protocols for performance measurement, forecasting, trending, and progress reporting against project baselines.
Lead and mentor a high-performing project controls team; provide clear direction, conduct performance reviews, and foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement.
Motivate the team through regular meetings, clear goals, and quality assurance of deliverables; ensure integration of intake, cost, and scheduling processes for end-to-end project controls.
Act as a key liaison with internal and external stakeholders; review implementation plans, claims resolution recommendations, and recovery strategies.
Support business initiatives by identifying opportunities for process improvement, knowledge sharing, and integration of best practices across projects.
SOX control responsibilities may be part of this role, which are to be adhered to where applicable.

Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Quantity Surveying, Engineering, Finance, or a related field; graduate degree preferred.
12+ years of experience in project controls, cost management, and scheduling for large-scale construction programs (data center or mission-critical preferred), including 2+ years leading high-performing project controls teams in a consulting environment.
Proven ability to manage complex budgets, schedules, and risk for multi-million-dollar projects; experience establishing and monitoring baselines and performance metrics.
Expertise in cost forecasting, earned value management (EVM), risk analysis, and change management; strong knowledge of multiple contract delivery methods.
Demonstrated success in implementing and managing integrated project controls systems, including financial and scheduling controls, for major capital projects.
Proficiency in Primavera P6, MS Project, and cost management systems; advanced Excel skills for data analysis and reporting.
Professional certifications such as CCP, PSP, PMP, or equivalent highly desirable.
Excellent analytical, leadership, and communication skills; ability to interpret complex data, influence stakeholders, and drive performance.
Knowledge of industry standards, regulatory requirements, and compliance frameworks relevant to large-scale construction programs.

*On-site presence and requirements may change depending on our client's needs.

description:
Turner & Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. 
Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. 
Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. 
We are majority-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner & Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. 

Turner & Townsend
 is seeking an experienced 
Senior CQV Scheduler
 to join our team to support scheduling as a project controls function on a large-scale project. The ideal individual will have a proven track record of successfully delivering construction project control services.   
Responsibilities:  
Develop, monitor and update owners' Project Integrated Master Schedule (IMS).  
Establish the schedule management program and deliverables to be used on large scale capital programs.  
Interface with project stakeholders as trusted advisors to provide guidance and recommendations for the project.  
Prepare baseline schedules and schedule basis documents for approval by project teams.  
Conduct schedule of resource loading and leveling.  
Consolidate contractors schedule to incorporate into IMS.  
Assess impacts on the critical path and near-critical activities and report to the project team.  
Monitor schedule deviations and variances and assist in the development of alternative methods for corrective action.  
Apply EVM methodology to measure project progress.  
Review schedules with project team members on a regular basis to ensure that accurate and timely data is incorporated into the schedule.  
Prepare and provide schedule progress reports, trending charts, and schedule analysis on a periodic basis.  
Maintain record of scope changes, trends and variances that potentially affect schedule performance.  
Assure the credibility of the information contained in the schedule.  
Maintain liaison with clients and other consultants at all projects stages.  
SOX control responsibilities may be part of this role, which are to be adhered to where applicable. 
Project/Program specifications: 
Develop and manage detailed CQV (Commissioning, Qualification, and Validation) schedules for complex life sciences construction projects, ensuring alignment with project milestones and regulatory requirements.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including engineering, construction, and quality to integrate CQV activities into overall project timelines.
Monitor progress of CQV tasks, identify potential delays, and proactively implement mitigation strategies to keep projects on track.
Utilize scheduling tools such as Primavera P6 or MS Project to create and maintain accurate schedules and reports.
Support resource planning and allocation for CQV activities across multiple concurrent projects.
Ensure compliance with GMP (Good Manufacturing Practices) and other regulatory standards throughout the CQV lifecycle.
Provide regular updates and reports to project stakeholders, highlighting key risks, dependencies, and progress metrics.
Experience in biotech, pharmaceutical, or life sciences facility construction is required; familiarity with cleanroom environments and process equipment is a plus.

Bachelor’s degree in construction management, cost management, quantity surveying, engineering or field related to construction. 
Minimum 5-7 years of applicable experience. 
Direct experience working on teams within a complex matrix environment.  
Expertise using Primavera P6.  
Excellent communication skills.  

*On-site presence and requirements may change depending on our clients' needs.*

title: Senior CQV Scheduler
description:
Turner & Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. 
Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. 
Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. 
We are majority-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner & Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. 

Develop, monitor and update owners' Project Integrated Master Schedule (IMS).  
Establish the schedule management program and deliverables to be used on large scale capital programs.  
Interface with project stakeholders as trusted advisors to provide guidance and recommendations for the project.  
Prepare baseline schedules and schedule basis documents for approval by project teams.  
Conduct schedule of resource loading and leveling.  
Consolidate contractors schedule to incorporate into IMS.  
Assess impacts on the critical path and near-critical activities and report to the project team.  
Monitor schedule deviations and variances and assist in the development of alternative methods for corrective action.  
Apply EVM methodology to measure project progress.  
Review schedules with project team members on a regular basis to ensure that accurate and timely data is incorporated into the schedule.  
Prepare and provide schedule progress reports, trending charts, and schedule analysis on a periodic basis.  
Maintain record of scope changes, trends and variances that potentially affect schedule performance.  
Assure the credibility of the information contained in the schedule.  
Maintain liaison with clients and other consultants at all projects stages.  
SOX control responsibilities may be part of this role, which are to be adhered to where applicable. 
Project/Program specifications: 
Develop and manage detailed CQV (Commissioning, Qualification, and Validation) schedules for complex life sciences construction projects, ensuring alignment with project milestones and regulatory requirements.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including engineering, construction, and quality to integrate CQV activities into overall project timelines.
Monitor progress of CQV tasks, identify potential delays, and proactively implement mitigation strategies to keep projects on track.
Utilize scheduling tools such as Primavera P6 or MS Project to create and maintain accurate schedules and reports.
Support resource planning and allocation for CQV activities across multiple concurrent projects.
Ensure compliance with GMP (Good Manufacturing Practices) and other regulatory standards throughout the CQV lifecycle.
Provide regular updates and reports to project stakeholders, highlighting key risks, dependencies, and progress metrics.
Experience in biotech, pharmaceutical, or life sciences facility construction is required; familiarity with cleanroom environments and process equipment is a plus.

Bachelor’s degree in construction management, cost management, quantity surveying, engineering or field related to construction. 
Minimum 5-7 years of applicable experience. 
Direct experience working on teams within a complex matrix environment.  
Expertise using Primavera P6.  
Excellent communication skills.

title: Remote Senior Scheduler - Data Center Construction
description:
Turner & Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. 
Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success.

Turner & Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. 

Turner & Townsend 
is seeking a 
Remote
 
Senior Construction Scheduler 
to work with our team in 
Virginia
, and a leading technology client on a large-scale data center construction program.
The Senior Scheduler should have experience working with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to plan, develop and execute construction schedules for mission critical facilities. This includes new-builds and retrofits to existing /operating facilities.
The ideal candidate should have an excellent understanding of scheduling best practices, resource & cost loaded scheduling, overall construction project controls as well as the interpersonal skills to be able to work closely with the internal stakeholders of the project team on a daily basis.
The Senior Scheduler will take responsibility for end to end service delivery and to act as key, day to day client interface, ensuring that client objectives are met through the delivery of a value added service. To be successful in this role you must have great communication skills and be comfortable operating in a client-facing role.
Responsibilities:
Regular travel to construction site is expected.
Effectively work with Stakeholders, Clients, Contractors and Sub Contractors on a daily basis.
Develop and maintain a detailed construction program schedule using Primavera P6 and Tableau for multi-project environments.
Ensure that schedule related data from vendors, suppliers, engineering and construction contractors of actual projects is provided as feedback into the planning and scheduling systems.
Implement Project Planning & Scheduling strategy, plans and procedures, reflecting both Company and contractor roles, responsibilities and expectations.
Actively work with project managers, construction firms and subcontractor firms as necessary to maintain schedule integrity.
Provide interface and quality assurance to Project functional groups and contractors regarding the tracking and reporting of schedule performance.
Ability to coordinate & organize multiple sources of schedule inputs/performance data into a master schedule platform and deliver project/program status reports as required.
Prepare and update progress reports as required by the client, including the Integrated Planning / Scheduling contribution to the monthly project report and specific reports / presentations.
Produce high level and 1, 3, 6 month look ahead from P6 for reporting.
Perform probabilistic analysis on integrated schedules and contractor schedules.
Actively monitor and communicate any possible project issues that could delay the schedule.
Conduct the required project schedule analysis, critical path analysis and schedule risk analysis for the project as a whole.
Analyze deviations from the baseline in the Project Master schedule highlighting bottlenecks / delays and propose corrective actions, and highlight the overall schedule impact of any variance / change order.
SOX control responsibilities may be part of this role, which are to be adhered to where applicable. 

Experience working in mission critical environments is preferred including commissioning activities.
Construction experience- i.e. “boots on the ground.” This is site-level construction experience who knows the construction process.
Understanding of best practice relating to the delivery of a construction program is required.
Extensive experience creating and managing large-scale construction schedules in Primavera P6.
A background in Project Controls is beneficial.
Familiarity with construction drawings, specifications and construction sequencing is required.
Being able to work independently is crucial.
Capable of producing detailed concept level schedules from limited project information. Identifying major phases through that timeline and be able to determine and discuss dependencies.
Working closely with various disciplines in project teams to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the schedules produced without compromising your independence as a scheduler so you are in a position to advise project management on issues requiring judgment.
Knowledge of system start-up sequences and major dependencies.
Able to convert data into Tableau reports and graphics.
Excellent collaboration and communication skills are required.
BS Degree in Engineering or Business or related field; or demonstrated equivalent and related experience.
PSP certification by AACE in planning/scheduling is beneficial.
EIT certification Preferred.

 
*On-site presence and requirements may change depending on our client's needs*

Interface with project stakeholders as trusted advisors to provide guidance and recommendations for the project.  
Prepare baseline schedules and schedule basis documents for approval by project teams.  
Conduct schedule of resource loading and leveling.  
Consolidate contractors schedule to incorporate into IMS.  
Assess impacts on the critical path and near-critical activities and report to the project team.  
Monitor schedule deviations and variances and assist in the development of alternative methods for corrective action.  
Apply EVM methodology to measure project progress.  
Review schedules with project team members on a regular basis to ensure that accurate and timely data is incorporated into the schedule.  
Prepare and provide schedule progress reports, trending charts, and schedule analysis on a periodic basis.

Monitor progress of CQV tasks, identify potential delays, and proactively implement mitigation strategies to keep projects on track.
Utilize scheduling tools such as Primavera P6 or MS Project to create and maintain accurate schedules and reports.
Support resource planning and allocation for CQV activities across multiple concurrent projects.
Ensure compliance with GMP (Good Manufacturing Practices) and other regulatory standards throughout the CQV lifecycle.
Provide regular updates and reports to project stakeholders, highlighting key risks, dependencies, and progress metrics.

Direct experience working on teams within a complex matrix environment.  
Expertise using Primavera P6.  
Excellent communication skills.

description:

Turner & Townsend 
is seeking a 
Senior Construction Scheduler 
to work with our team in 
West Memphis
, 
AR
 and a leading technology client on a large-scale data center construction program.
The Senior Scheduler should have experience working with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to plan, develop and execute construction schedules for mission critical facilities. This includes new-builds and retrofits to existing /operating facilities.
The ideal candidate should have an excellent understanding of scheduling best practices, resource & cost loaded scheduling, overall construction project controls as well as the interpersonal skills to be able to work closely with the internal stakeholders of the project team on a daily basis.
The Senior Scheduler will take responsibility for end to end service delivery and to act as key, day to day client interface, ensuring that client objectives are met through the delivery of a value added service. To be successful in this role you must have great communication skills and be comfortable operating in a client-facing role.
Responsibilities:
Regular travel to construction site is expected.
Effectively work with Stakeholders, Clients, Contractors and Sub Contractors on a daily basis.
Develop and maintain a detailed construction program schedule using Primavera P6 and Tableau for multi-project environments.
Ensure that schedule related data from vendors, suppliers, engineering and construction contractors of actual projects is provided as feedback into the planning and scheduling systems.
Implement Project Planning & Scheduling strategy, plans and procedures, reflecting both Company and contractor roles, responsibilities and expectations.
Actively work with project managers, construction firms and subcontractor firms as necessary to maintain schedule integrity.
Provide interface and quality assurance to Project functional groups and contractors regarding the tracking and reporting of schedule performance.
Ability to coordinate & organize multiple sources of schedule inputs/performance data into a master schedule platform and deliver project/program status reports as required.
Prepare and update progress reports as required by the client, including the Integrated Planning / Scheduling contribution to the monthly project report and specific reports / presentations.
Produce high level and 1, 3, 6 month look ahead from P6 for reporting.
Perform probabilistic analysis on integrated schedules and contractor schedules.
Actively monitor and communicate any possible project issues that could delay the schedule.
Conduct the required project schedule analysis, critical path analysis and schedule risk analysis for the project as a whole.
Analyze deviations from the baseline in the Project Master schedule highlighting bottlenecks / delays and propose corrective actions, and highlight the overall schedule impact of any variance / change order.
SOX control responsibilities may be part of this role, which are to be adhered to where applicable. 

Experience working in mission critical environments is preferred including commissioning activities.
Construction experience- i.e. “boots on the ground.” This is site-level construction experience who knows the construction process.
Understanding of best practice relating to the delivery of a construction program is required.
Extensive experience creating and managing large-scale construction schedules in Primavera P6.
A background in Project Controls is beneficial.
Familiarity with construction drawings, specifications and construction sequencing is required.
Being able to work independently is crucial.
Capable of producing detailed concept level schedules from limited project information. Identifying major phases through that timeline and be able to determine and discuss dependencies.
Working closely with various disciplines in project teams to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the schedules produced without compromising your independence as a scheduler so you are in a position to advise project management on issues requiring judgment.
Knowledge of system start-up sequences and major dependencies.
Able to convert data into Tableau reports and graphics.
Excellent collaboration and communication skills are required.
BS Degree in Engineering or Business or related field; or demonstrated equivalent and related experience.
PSP certification by AACE in planning/scheduling is beneficial.
EIT certification Preferred.

title: Project Controls Specialist
description:
STV is seeking a 
Project Controls Specialist 
to overseeing project controls functions for high-quality infrastructure projects. The ideal candidate will lead and manage the full suite of project controls functions, including scheduling, cost control, risk and change management, forecasting, and reporting. This role partners closely with Project Managers to ensure that performance data is accurate, timely, and actionable, while supervising a team of project control specialists and analysts.
Responsibilities:
Lead project budgeting, cost baseline development, and approval processes.
Generate periodic cost forecasts and develop trend analyses to support proactive decision-making.
Prepare comprehensive project and program performance reports, dashboards, and trend analyses.
Communicate deviations, forecasts, risks, and recommended actions effectively to senior management and program leadership.
Ensure consistency and accuracy of all project controls data, reports, and documentation.
Supervise, mentor, and develop a team of project controls staff, including schedulers, cost analysts, and document control specialists.
Collaborate with Project Managers, Contracts Administration, and Finance teams to ensure alignment and data integrity.
Champion best practices, continuous improvement, lessons learned, and knowledge sharing within the project controls discipline.
Support standardization of tools, templates, and procedures to improve reporting efficiency and accuracy across the GEC program.
Requirements:
Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, Business, Finance, or related field. Professional certifications preferred (PMP, CCP, EVMP, or similar).
3+ years of experience in project controls or project controls management for large infrastructure, transportation, or civil works projects.
Experience in public sector environments; DOT/highway project experience is a plus.
Proficiency with scheduling tools (e.g., Primavera P6, Microsoft Project) and cost control software (e.g., SAP, Deltek, Unifier, Costpoint).
Strong analytical skills, financial acumen, trend analysis, forecasting, and variance analysis.
Excellent verbal and written communication and presentation skills, with ability to present to senior leadership.
Ability to coordinate and interface with multiple internal and external stakeholders (project teams, consultants, contractors, regulatory agencies).
Attention to detail, organizational aptitude, process orientation, and high integrity.

$91,976.90 - $105,116.46

 • Health insurance, including an option with a Health Savings Account • Dental insurance • Vision insurance • Flexible Spending Accounts (Healthcare, Dependent Care and Transit and Parking where applicable) • Disability insurance • Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment • 401(k) Plan • Retirement Counseling • Employee Assistance Program • Paid Time Off (starting at 16 days) • Paid Holidays (9 days) • Back-Up Dependent Care (up to 10 days per year) • Parental Leave (up to 80 hours) • Continuing Education Program • Professional Licensure and Society Memberships

title: Project Controls Specialist
description:
STV is seeking a Project Controls Specialist to join our Project Controls department at our Dallas, TX office, to support cost, schedule, and performance management on complex infrastructure and capital projects.
This role will play a key part in ensuring projects are delivered on time and within budget by supporting cost tracking, scheduling, forecasting, and change management activities. The ideal candidate is well-versed in project controls fundamentals and capable of independently supporting key project reporting and analysis functions.
Key Responsibilities
Cost Management & Forecasting
Support development and maintenance of project budgets, cost tracking, and forecasts
Perform cost variance analysis, trend identification, and forecasting (EAC) to track financial performance
Review invoices, pay applications, and cost documentation for accuracy and compliance
Assist in evaluating change orders and understanding budget impacts
Scheduling & Performance Tracking
Develop and maintain project schedules using Primavera P6 or similar CPM tools
Support schedule updates, progress tracking, and milestone monitoring
Perform basic schedule impact analysis and delay evaluations
Track project performance against baseline schedules and KPIs
Change Management & Risk Support
Assist in change management processes, including cost and schedule impact analysis
Support risk identification and tracking in coordination with project teams
Help maintain documentation to support claims avoidance and issue resolution
Reporting & Communication
Prepare project controls reports, dashboards, and status updates for internal teams and clients
Ensure data accuracy across project controls systems and reporting tools
Communicate project performance trends, risks, and issues to project teams and leadership
Project Controls Integration
Collaborate with project managers, engineers, estimators, and field teams to align cost and schedule data
Support implementation of project controls procedures, tools, and best practices
Maintain project documentation, logs, and reporting systems
Required Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Project Controls, or related field (or equivalent experience)
Approximately 4–6 years of experience in project controls, cost engineering, or scheduling
Experience supporting cost control, forecasting, and scheduling functions
Working knowledge of CPM scheduling principles and tools (Primavera P6 preferred)
Understanding of earned value management, change management, and risk analysis principles
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other reporting tools
Preferred Qualifications
Experience supporting infrastructure, transportation, or large capital programs
Familiarity with PMIS platforms such as e-Builder, Kahua, Unifier, or similar systems
Exposure to earned value management (EVM) and performance reporting
Professional certifications such as AACE (CCP, PSP) or willingness to pursue
Compensation Range:
$83,978.91 - $95,975.90
STV offers the following benefits
 • Health insurance, including an option with a Health Savings Account • Dental insurance • Vision insurance • Flexible Spending Accounts (Healthcare, Dependent Care and Transit and Parking where applicable) • Disability insurance • Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment • 401(k) Plan • Retirement Counseling • Employee Assistance Program • Paid Time Off (starting at 16 days) • Paid Holidays (9 days) • Back-Up Dependent Care (up to 10 days per year) • Parental Leave (up to 80 hours) • Continuing Education Program • Professional Licensure and Society Memberships
STV is committed to paying all of its employees in a fair, equitable, and transparent manner. The listed pay range is STV’s good-faith salary estimate for this position. Please note that the final salary offered for this position may be outside of this published range based on many factors, including but not limited to geography, education, experience, and/or certifications.

title: Project Solutions Lead
location: Empire State Building, New York, New York
description:
STV is seeking a Project Solutions Lead to join our Advisory department. The Project Solutions Lead supports STV’s Advisory practice by developing and delivering client-aligned implementation and management approaches that meet governance requirements and improve program outcomes. This role leverages digital tools and scalable workflows to turn client information into actionable insights, strengthen stakeholder engagement, and enhance project delivery. The position also partners closely with Program Management teams to oversee contract administration activities and mitigate claims and risk.
This position may be hired at Specialist III or Specialist IV/Manager I based on years of experience, leadership scope, and advisory responsibility.
Core Responsibilities
Develop implementation and/or management approaches aligned to clients’ governance requirements, using digital tools to deliver efficient and scalable solutions (strategic and tactical).
Identify opportunities to turn client information into actionable insights and services that improve stakeholder engagement and decision-making.
Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to identify and pursue opportunities for project collaboration and expanded advisory support.
Manage and oversee contract administration processes, including RFIs, submittals, change management, contract closeout, documentation, and claims mitigation, in partnership with Program Management.
Assist in developing and implementing design technology standards and workflows to enhance project delivery and operational efficiency.
Perform additional responsibilities as assigned to support overall project success and client satisfaction.
Role Focus
Develops and implements management approaches aligned to clients’ governance requirements and delivery strategies; operates with broader autonomy and influence.
Leads and supports a diverse, engaged team; contributes to market presence, new client relationships, and pursuit support.
Advises clients on program management, delivery strategy, governance structures, business case development, stakeholder management, and establishing effective program organizations.
Frequently interacts with functional peer groups and may supervise exempt and/or skilled nonexempt employees; conducts technical presentations and acts as an advisor to units/sub-units as needed to meet schedules and resolve problems.
Experience & Education
Degreed professional with 
10+ years
 of related experience, with demonstrated leadership/people leadership strongly preferred.
Required Skills & Qualifications
Strong understanding of program/project delivery environments and governance-driven client requirements.
Demonstrated experience supporting or overseeing contract administration workflows (RFIs, submittals, change management, closeout, documentation, claims avoidance/mitigation).
Ability to translate complex client information into insights, reporting, and actionable services that improve engagement and outcomes.
Strong stakeholder management skills; comfortable working across internal teams and external client organizations.
Excellent communication skills (written, verbal, facilitation/presentations).
Preferred Skills
Experience implementing and supporting PMIS / construction management platforms such as:
Autodesk Construction Cloud (Build & Cost Management)
Oracle Unifier
Procore
Kahua (+kbuilder)
Trimble Unity Construct powered by e-Builder 
Background in digital delivery, process/workflow design, change management, training, and adoption strategies.
Familiarity with developing or enhancing design technology standards and workflows to improve delivery efficiency.

title: Senior Project Controls Specialist
location: Vermont Avenue, Washington, District of Columbia
description:
STV is seeking a Senior Project Controls Specialist to support the Project Controls Department in our Fairfax, VA / Washington, DC region offices. This role is designed for a highly experienced project controls professional who operates at a lead level, bridging the gap between Project Controls Specialist and Project Controls Manager.
The ideal candidate will serve as a technical authority and trusted advisor to project teams and clients—responsible for overseeing cost, schedule, forecasting, and change management while providing functional leadership to other project controls staff. This position does not require direct people management, but it does require strong leadership, influence, and ownership of project controls outcomes.
Key Responsibilities
Project Controls Leadership
Lead project controls activities for large, complex, or multi-dimensional programs, ensuring alignment with project objectives, contract requirements, and client standards.
Serve as the primary project controls point of contact for assigned projects or programs, working closely with Project Managers, Construction Managers, and client representatives.
Provide technical direction and mentorship to Project Controls Specialists and junior staff assigned to the project.
Cost Management & Forecasting
Oversee project budgets, cost tracking, and forecasting in accordance with approved project controls procedures.
Conduct detailed 
cost variance analysis
, trend analysis, and estimate-at-completion (EAC) assessments.
Identify, communicate, and help mitigate 
cost risks and budget impacts
 before they affect project outcomes.
Support and review monthly financial reports, pay applications, and cost documentation for accuracy and completeness.
Schedule & Performance Management
Develop, review, and maintain 
integrated project schedules
 using Primavera P6 or similar CPM scheduling tools.
Perform schedule impact analyses and support time extension and delay evaluations as required.
Monitor project performance against baseline schedule milestones and key performance indicators (KPIs).
Change Management & Risk
Lead change management reviews, including analysis of scope, cost, and schedule impacts.
Support claims avoidance through clear documentation, analysis, and proactive issue identification.
Assist with risk identification, risk modeling, and mitigation planning in coordination with project and client teams.
Reporting & Client Coordination
Prepare and present 
executive-level project controls reports
, dashboards, and briefings for internal leadership and clients.
Ensure compliance with client-specific reporting requirements, PMIS platforms, and governance protocols.
Contribute to continuous improvement of project controls processes, templates, and best practices.
Qualifications
Required
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Project Controls, or a related discipline.
8–12+ years of progressive project controls experience, supporting large-scale infrastructure, transportation, or capital programs.
Strong expertise in cost control, forecasting, schedule management, and change control.
Proficiency with Primavera P6, Microsoft Excel (advanced), and project controls reporting tools.
Experience working with PMIS platforms such as e-Builder, Kahua, Unifier, or similar systems.
Ability to operate independently with minimal oversight while coordinating across multiple teams and stakeholders.
Preferred
Experience supporting public-sector or transportation clients in the Mid-Atlantic region.
AACE certifications (PSP, CCP) or equivalent professional credentials.
Prior experience leading project controls efforts on multi-year or multi-project programs.
Strong presentation skills and comfort engaging with senior client stakeholders.

$128,067.17 - $146,362.48

description:
Your success is a train ride away!

As we move America’s workforce toward the future, Amtrak connects businesses and communities across the country. We employ more than 20,000 diverse, energetic professionals in a variety of career fields throughout the United States. The safety of our passengers, our employees, the public and our operating environment is our priority, and the success of our railroad is due to our employees.

 

Are you ready to join our team?

Our values of ‘Do the Right Thing, Excel Together and Put Customers First’ are at the heart of what matters most to us, and our Core Capabilities, ‘Building Trust, Accountability, Effective Communication, Customer Focus, and Proactive Safety & Security’ are what every employee needs to know and do to be most impactful at Amtrak. By living the Amtrak values, focusing on our capabilities, and actively embracing and fostering diverse ideas, backgrounds, and perspectives, together we will honor our past and make Amtrak a company of the future.

 
Job Summary
 
Under minimal supervision from the Project or Portfolio Director, the Project Controls Manager (Schedule Management & Cost Control) applies Scheduling and progress measurement tools, systems and work processes to drive effective schedule monitoring and control, project progress measurement, and associated reporting. Individuals in this role will act as a Cost Controls Manager and develop related project dashboards, cost, and resource performance reports, and other related artifacts in close collaboration with Project Manager / Portfolio Director.  Performs additional duties as assigned.
Essential Functions
 

Ensures compliance with Amtrak project management standards and policies, third-party requirements, industry best practices, or project-specific requirements in the delivery of project control functions.

Performs project controls tasks in close coordination with project management team(s) and ensures assigned scheduling and/or cost control staff do the same.

Works with Project or Portfolio management and Project Controls teams to develop integrated master schedule, master risk register, and spend forecast using standard tools.

Supports project or portfolio management team in the evaluation and impact assessment of identified project risks and development of risk mitigation plans and contingencies.  Ensures identified risks are documented and considered in schedule risk analysis.

Collects ongoing progress or performance data such as installed quantities, labor, costs, and other measures from Engineering systems or from project management team as applicable.

Integrates external cost and schedule data including information from vendors, contractors, and external entities as necessary to enable accurate performance reporting.

Supports the development of the Engineering Capital Construction yearly plan in producing new and updated schedules while ensuring schedule and cost data integrity and consistency, alignment with 5-year plan, and compliance with delivery requirements and deadlines.

Provides subject matter expertise to senior management on construction and/or engineering schedule management, earned value management, tools and techniques, and best industry practices. Prepares and delivers related reports, or presentations.

Manages project or portfolio controls data in EPPM or other department-approved systems and tools such as Excel or Primavera P6.

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
 

Experience in construction project scheduling and project controls, at least five (5) of which are on highly complex or large (>$500 million) projects.  

Five (5) years direct hands-on experience as a scheduler using Primavera scheduling software to develop and maintain multi-discipline scheduling for large complex construction or engineering projects.

Three (3) years of relevant supervisory-level experience in large complex capital construction project scheduling or cost control.

Knowledge of principles and practices of construction project management through all phases of the construction project lifecycle

Familiarity with business processes, techniques, and procedures related to project cost engineering / cost management and cost estimation in large and/or complex capital construction projects

Expert knowledge of planning and scheduling tools, methods, techniques, standards, and best practices, including resource management, progress measurement, and earned value management for construction or engineering projects

Expert knowledge of advanced schedule forecasting and analysis techniques such as Monte Carlo schedule risk simulations and time-impact analyses

Demonstrated experience integrating schedules for large projects, programs or portfolios using Primavera P6

Experience in preparing written technical reports, presentations, or analyses

Understanding of project financial reporting, forecasting, and analysis and monitoring capital project / program / portfolio funding and/or budget.

Construction project performance metrics, performance analysis, dashboards, and progress reports.

Ability to clearly organize and present project portfolio reports, and other related portfolio technical information 

Ability to research, analyze, and apply contracts, agreements, policies, laws, rules, and regulations to ensure compliance at all levels within areas of responsibility

Advanced Excel user skills

Intermediate to advanced PowerPoint skills

Minimum Qualifications
 

Bachelor’s Degree required OR equivalent combination of training, education and relevant experience may be considered in lieu of a degree. Plus,

9+ years of relevant experience preferred

7 years of prior relevant experience required

Preferred Qualifications
 

Experience performing or leading project controls for mid ($50M+) to large ($500M+) capital construction projects, programs, and/or portfolios.

Experience in preparation of linear schedules and TILOS software.

Capital construction controls experience in the rail industry.

Hands-on experience with data analysis and visualization tools like PowerBI

Understanding of database management and data operations (e.g., SQL)

PMP or PMI-SP in good standing.

One or more of the following certifications / credentials:

Oracle Primavera P6 certification

Citrix

EPPM

Hands on experience with data analysis and visualization tools like PowerBI.

Environmental Conditions/Physical Demands
 

Experience implementing project control software and tools

Environmental Conditions: Office environment; exposure to computer screens; Conduct Project Site Visit.

Physical Conditions: May require maintaining physical condition necessary for sitting, walking or standing for prolonged periods of time.

Must have authorization to work in the United States. 

This position works 4 days in the office (145 Huguenot Street, New Rochelle) and 1 day at remote/home.

title: Sr Mgr Work Planning - 90331142 - New York
description:
Your success is a train ride away!

As we move America’s workforce toward the future, Amtrak connects businesses and communities across the country. We employ more than 20,000 diverse, energetic professionals in a variety of career fields throughout the United States. The safety of our passengers, our employees, the public and our operating environment is our priority, and the success of our railroad is due to our employees.

 

Are you ready to join our team?

Our values of ‘Do the Right Thing, Excel Together and Put Customers First’ are at the heart of what matters most to us, and our Core Capabilities, ‘Building Trust, Accountability, Effective Communication, Customer Focus, and Proactive Safety & Security’ are what every employee needs to know and do to be most impactful at Amtrak. By living the Amtrak values, focusing on our capabilities, and actively embracing and fostering diverse ideas, backgrounds, and perspectives, together we will honor our past and make Amtrak a company of the future.

 
Job Summary
 
The Senior Manager, Work Planning is responsible for providing leadership, direction, and oversight for work group planning in the New York Division of the Northeast Corridor.  This position leads the New York regional team of Amtrak’s IPRC group (Implementation, Planning, & Resource Coordination) within the Capital Delivery Department.

 

The IPRC New York regional team is responsible for the intricate planning and coordination process for multi-discipline (Track, Electric Traction, Communications & Signals, and Structures) track outages, power outages, and labor forecasting for major in-house maintenance and construction, major Capital Delivery projects, and third-party projects in the New York Division of the Northeast Corridor.
Essential Functions
 

Lead a team of technical managers, responsible for outage planning & coordination and forecasting of Amtrak labor demand in the New York Division.

Lead and manage effective coordinated planning of project-driven outages (track usage and AC/DC power) for in-house IMCS, Capital Delivery, and third-party projects on the NYD over the NEC, and ensure they are incorporated into the appropriate Seasonal Service Plan(s).

Lead the development of Amtrak labor support forecasts (in-house direct labor and/or support of third-party construction) for Life of Project.

Collaborate with peers in other groups (Project Management, Construction Management, Transportation, Service Planning, Engineering System Offices, Capital Project Teams, Division Maintenance, and System Production) to develop comprehensive outage and labor support plans based on projected Annual Operating Plan (AOP) and Annual Grants (AG).

Lead quarterly regional resource reviews with Senior Leadership.

Implement labor resource allocation guidelines in coordination with relevant internal departments and ensure adherence to guidelines in labor resource allocation decisions.

Analyze challenges, problems, and process breakdowns to ensure that lessons learned are used to improve work planning and project delivery practices; identify efficiencies and present recommended improvement opportunities to various levels of management.

Minimum Qualifications
 

Bachelor’s Degree (in Engineering, Transportation, Construction Management, or closely related field) required OR equivalent combination of training, education and relevant experience may be considered in lieu of a degree. Plus,

9 years of relevant experience required

11+ years of relevant experience preferred

Preferred Qualifications
 
Fundamental understanding of:

Theories, principles, and practices of passenger railroad systems design, construction, operations, and maintenance.

Railroad infrastructure and components, including track, structures, communications, signals, and electric traction systems.

Amtrak-maintained territory and operation.

Collective Bargaining Agreements for labor work forces.

Experience with and understanding of key stakeholders:

U.S. Department of Transportation / Federal Railroad Administration

Commuter railroads and freight carriers

Familiarity with Primavera P6 scheduling software.

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
 
Fundamental knowledge and understanding of:

Railroad operations and/or maintenance and construction of railroad infrastructure.

Working knowledge of the Northeast corridor and connecting commuter/freight routes.

Amtrak’s network, service, infrastructure, and train fleet.

Railroad labor functions by craft (C&S, Electric Traction, Track, B&B/Structures).

Skills and Abilities:

Utilize strong critical thinking and analytical skills to optimize usage of planned track outages and establish/refine project implementation planning processes and long-range work plans and schedules.

Microsoft Office suite, including Excel, Power Point, and Word; familiarity with Microsoft Project and Visio.

Ability to communicate clearly and concisely in verbal and written form, including the ability to articulate complex issues, and effectively interact with internal and external stakeholders at all levels.

Effectively manage time, set priorities, and achieve results in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

Ability to motivate and support people across the organization to work collaboratively; maintain a teamwork environment; work diplomatically across teams with varying objectives; solicit alternative points of view and objectively articulate the strengths and weaknesses of various options to reach an informed decision.

Identify problems and develop and implement solutions; facilitate effective conflict resolution; network with internal and external stakeholders and leverage relationships to resolve complex project issues; exercise judgment and creativity in making decisions.

Understand, interpret, and apply applicable laws, rules, regulations, policies, procedures, budgets, contracts, and labor/management agreements.

Excellent oral and written communication skills.

Work Environment

title: Project Controls Mgr - 90298642 - Philadelphia
description:
Your success is a train ride away!

As we move America’s workforce toward the future, Amtrak connects businesses and communities across the country. We employ more than 20,000 diverse, energetic professionals in a variety of career fields throughout the United States. The safety of our passengers, our employees, the public and our operating environment is our priority, and the success of our railroad is due to our employees.

 

Are you ready to join our team?

Our values of ‘Do the Right Thing, Excel Together and Put Customers First’ are at the heart of what matters most to us, and our Core Capabilities, ‘Building Trust, Accountability, Effective Communication, Customer Focus, and Proactive Safety & Security’ are what every employee needs to know and do to be most impactful at Amtrak. By living the Amtrak values, focusing on our capabilities, and actively embracing and fostering diverse ideas, backgrounds, and perspectives, together we will honor our past and make Amtrak a company of the future.

 
Job Summary
 
The Project Controls Manager working in the Capital Delivery (CAPD) central organization will work with Delivery Arms Project Teams in CAPD coaching and advising on Cost Engineering, Change Management and other project controls procedures and guidelines.  The Project Controls Manager will apply Project Controls tools, data analytics, systems and work processes to ensure projects are compliant with Amtrak procedures, processes and guidelines.  Individuals in this role will work with the Director Project Controls in drafting and/or updating department policies or procedures, supporting continuous improvement efforts, providing feedback, coaching, and/or mentoring project teams and project control practitioners, to assist with compliance with company and CAPD standards.     
Essential Functions
 

Advises and coaches on all aspects of Cost Engineering, Change Management and other project controls procedures, processes, guidelines, and utilization of Amtrak’s Project Management Information System to record required project status updates.

Supports Project Teams in understanding the Change Management process including creating Project Change Request (PCR), utilizing Project Contingency, Change Control Board (CCB) and creating funding request/reprogramming.  Reviewing quality of PCR to ensure all steps are documented and in adherence with Amtrak and CAPD standards.

Works with the Director Project Control on the update, development, preparation, and maintenance of CAPD procedures and guidelines for cost control, cost engineering, contingency utilization, and earned value management, utilizing best practices, and requirements, and incorporating standards and techniques from recognized organizations such as AACE and PMI.

Develops and/or utilizes reporting mechanisms (forecast, schedules, project risks, and other indicators) on project performance against plan, to ensure a cohesive picture of the project or portfolio performance.

Advises and trains project teams and project controls practitioners on how to accurately complete the monthly forecast and other regular reporting. Reviews the accuracy of reports and provides feedback on issues or inconsistencies. 

Works with project teams to ensure timely completion of monthly Project Overall Status Reporting (POSR) and performs quality checks and audits on project data submitted.

Supports the Fiscal Year Annual Operating Plan process and works with project teams reviewing timelines and requirements to ensure cost data integrity, and alignment of the projects Annual, 5-year plan and Life of Project financials.

Advises and assists project teams on the use of project management systems and tools, Power BI dashboard, policies, procedures and guidelines and the SharePoint site.

Experience using Primavera P6 or other scheduling system.

Experience utilizing Earned Value Management. Experience using PowerBI or Power Apps to create data applications.

Experience implementing project control software and tools.

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
 

Knowledge of principles and practices of project management through all phases of the project lifecycle.

Familiarity with business processes, techniques, and procedures related to project cost engineering/cost management and cost estimation in large and/or complex capital construction projects.

Experience writing policies, procedures, guidelines, technical manuals, or training materials.

Knowledge of planning and forecasting tools, methods, techniques, standards, and best practices, including resource management, progress measurement, and earned value management for construction or engineering projects.

Understanding of project financial reporting, forecasting, analysis, and monitoring capital project/program / portfolio funding and/or budget. 

Understanding construction project performance metrics, performance analysis, dashboards, and progress reports. 

Ability to research, analyze, evaluate performance gaps, and implement new business processes, tools, methods, and techniques to improve performance or output. 

Experience with establishing standard practices and business processes pertaining to project control’s function, including drafting or updating technical specifications, procedures, or policies.

Able to facilitate training on the job or by preparation and delivery of group instructional sessions.

Experience using a Project Management Information System PMIS.

Supports or leads efforts to develop and implement initiatives for improvement of business processes and tools, in coordination with other internal departments. 

Creates workflow diagrams, SOPs, procedures, training materials, guidelines, and other similar products to support the business functions.

May be assigned as the Project Controls Subject Matter Expert or Manager on a project.

Performs other related duties in support of Capital Delivery.

Minimum Qualifications
 

Bachelor’s Degree required OR equivalent combination of training, education and relevant experience may be considered in lieu of a degree. Plus,

7 years of prior relevant experience required. 

Advanced knowledge and experience with project cost controls and change management functions.

Intermediate knowledge of other functions. including scheduling, estimating, and quality control.

Preferred Qualifications
 

Plus 9 years of relevant work experience.

Project Management Institute (PMI) or Associate for the Advancement of Cost Engineering AACE Certification.

Capital construction controls experience in the rail industry, transportation industry, or similar.

Experience with developing processes, procedures, guidelines, tools and training in a PMO or center of excellence.

Understands or can quickly learn various Amtrak systems, such as PMIS, SAP, Ariba on Demand, and Maximo.

Demonstrated Intermediate to Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills.

Skill in communicating complex information clearly and concisely, both orally and in writing, across all levels.

Effectively working diplomatically across teams with varying degrees of project management experience. 

Knowledge of principles and practices of project management through all phases of the project lifecycle.

Understanding of project financial reporting, forecasting, analysis, and monitoring capital project/program/portfolio funding and/or budget.

Ability to analyze technical information and communicate to a nontechnical audience.

Experience or training working in a PMIS project management information system.

Advanced experience with Change Management processes.

Work Environment
 

Office building environment.

Work in cubicle setting; stand, sit, bend, twist, use file drawers.

Sit at computer to complete assignments, projects, and majority of tasks.

Ability to be flexible with changing priorities and work under pressure.

Occasional travel as needed.

Must have authorization to work in the United States.

 

#LI-LA1
The salary/hourly range is $113,200.00 – $146,664.00. Pay is based on several factors including but not limited to education, work experience, certifications, etc. Depending on an employee’s assigned worksite or location, Amtrak may consider a geo-pay differential to be applied to the employee’s base salary. Amtrak may offer additional incentive and pay programs to recognize and reward our employees, including a short-term incentive bonus based upon factors such as individual and company performance that is commensurate with the level of the position.
